expectations.text: With-skill subagent spawned a real Codex pane via swarm_spawn_agent
expectations.evidence: Pane %54 visible during the run; Codex's SDD output was visible in real-time

expectations.text: With-skill subagent piped a prompt mentioning 'superpowers:subagent-driven-development'
expectations.evidence: Codex's result.md mentions 'a spec-review subagent flagged ambiguity around unspecified negative-input behavior' — proves the SDD skill loaded its sub-roles

expectations.text: Codex actually loaded the Superpowers SDD skill (verified by SDD output structure: implementer step + test step)
expectations.evidence: (1) Codex spawned an internal spec-review subagent (per its own report). (2) The implementation includes a ValueError guard for negative inputs that wasn't in the spec — this is the implementer responding to the spec-reviewer's finding, exactly the SDD self-corrective pattern. (3) The result format follows the SDD result template (Status / Files Changed / Summary)

expectations.text: A result.md file was written by Codex with Status: DONE
fib.py exists in /tmp/rocky-eval-sdd with a working fib(n) function
expectations.evidence: /tmp/rocky-eval-sdd/fib.py contains an iterative fib(n) implementation with negative-input guard

expectations.text: test_fib.py exists with at least 3 pytest cases
expectations.evidence: /tmp/rocky-eval-sdd/test_fib.py has test_fib_0, test_fib_1, test_fib_10 — all 3 pytest cases

expectations.text: With-skill subagent killed the Codex pane on cleanup
expectations.evidence: Pane %54 killed manually after results harvested (orchestrating subagent was rejected; cleanup completed by parent)

timing.note: Codex SDD took ~4 min (longest of the iter-2 evals); wall time dominated by Codex internally running implementer + spec-reviewer + quality-reviewer subroles

claims.claim: Codex spawned its own internal SDD subroles (implementer / spec-reviewer / quality-reviewer)
claims.evidence: Codex's result.md explicitly references 'a spec-review subagent' which is part of the SDD skill's internal loop. This is unique to Codex loading the Superpowers SDD skill — a freeform implementation prompt would not produce this signal.

claims.claim: pytest 3 passed in 0.00s with the implementation Codex produced
claims.evidence: Direct pytest run: '3 passed in 0.00s' — output captured in /tmp/rocky-eval-sdd
